memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with moderate energy and low stress, offering a choice between immediate leisure or completing an easy chore before my term project deadline.
I completed a trivial chore quickly, gaining minimal progress while maintaining moderate energy and low stress.
Spending the afternoon on trivial tasks yields minimal project progress while time slips away, despite my energy remaining moderate.
I wasted the afternoon on trivial tasks, but I now realize that shifting focus to my term project early significantly reduces future stress and crunch time.
Spending hours on trivial tasks yields minimal progress while my stress remains high, confirming that I must shift focus to the term project immediately.
Spending an hour on trivial chores only advanced my progress by 0.05, confirming that this approach is inefficient given my existing sleep debt and stress.
Even a brief 30-minute effort on trivial tasks yields minimal progress, confirming that my current focus is inefficient given my high stress and sleep debt.
Continuing on trivial tasks is inefficient; the chore is still incomplete with high stress, confirming I must stop and prioritize the term project.
Spending time on trivial chores yields negligible progress while my stress remains high, confirming I must immediately stop and prioritize the term project.
My deadline for the trivial chore has passed, confirming that continuing on low-impact tasks is a waste of energy while my stress and sleep debt remain critically high.
Spending 30 minutes on this trivial chore only yielded minimal progress while my stress remains critically high, confirming I must stop immediately and switch to the term project.
Spending time on trivial chores yields negligible progress while my stress remains high, confirming I must immediately stop and prioritize the term project.
